mission.title: containerd: containerd builds for arm64
mission.description: containerd builds for arm64. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current containerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your containerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
helm list -n containerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working containerd installation.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
There are prebuilt binaries for amd64. As arm64 grows in popularity for building micro Kubernetes clusters, it would be nice to be able to pick arm64 builds of containerd instead of needing to install docker, or spend a lot of time building

steps.title: Apply the fix for containerd builds for arm64
steps.description:
This patch adds the CI configuration to enable the support for arm build in OpenLab.

After this, each pull request in containerd will trigger the ``containerd-arm64-build`` job which verified the arm build on ARM cluster.

As the first version, we mark this job as non-voting job, that means the
```yaml
kind: pipeline
name: default

platform:
  os: linux
  arch: arm64

workspace:
  base: /go
  path: src/github.com/containerd/containerd

steps:
- name: build
  image: golang:1.10.3
  commands:
  - apt-get update
  - apt-get install -y btrfs-tools libseccomp-dev
  - make
  - make test
```

steps.title: Upgrade containerd to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade containerd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade containerd containerd/containerd --namespace containerd
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd
helm list -n containerd
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "containerd builds for arm64" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This patch adds the CI configuration to enable the support for arm build in OpenLab.

After this, each pull request in containerd will trigger the ``containerd-arm64-build`` job which verified the arm build on ARM cluster.

As the first version, we mark this job as non-voting job, that means the build job result is just a reference for developer and would not block the PR to be merged.
codeSnippets:
kind: pipeline
name: default

platform:
  os: linux
  arch: arm64

workspace:
  base: /go
  path: src/github.com/containerd/containerd

steps:
- name: build
  image: golang:1.10.3
  commands:
  - apt-get update
  - apt-get install -y btrfs-tools libseccomp-dev
  - make
  - make test

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
